2605.23470.title: Learning Individual Dynamics from Sparse Cross-Sectional Snapshots
2605.23470.paper_summary: Predicting how a dynamical unit evolves over time - how an individual ages, an epidemic spreads, or a physical system degrades - typically requires dense longitudinal tracking. When only extremely sparse or entirely cross-sectional data is available, inferring individualized, continuous-time trajectories is fundamentally ill-posed. Existing methods force a strict compromise: sequence models (e.g. latent ODEs) require dense longitudinal data, while cross-sectional methods (e.g. optimal transport, flow matching-based) map aggregate populations, losing individual dynamics. In this paper, we demonstrate that this dichotomy can be broken. We introduce CADENCE, a principled probabilistic framework that recovers continuous individual trajectories from isolated snapshots by anchoring latent dynamics to static, individual-level contexts. We provide novel identifiability guarantees for single-timepoint trajectory inference. By combining a score-based spatial encoder (bijective Probability Flow ODE) to eliminate diffeomorphic ambiguities with a Soft Mixture-of-Experts (SMoE) router, we show that individual dynamical parameters and routing function are jointly identifiable. Across a suite of benchmarks spanning physical systems to real-world biological data, CADENCE, trained strictly on extremely sparse snapshots with context structure, matches or exceeds the performance of state-of-the-art sequential models trained on dense, full-trajectory data.
2605.23470.paper_summary_zh: 預測一個動態單元如何隨時間演變 - 如何個體老化、流行病擴散或物理系統退化 - 通常需要密集的縱向追蹤。當僅有極度稀疏或完全橫斷面的數據可用時，推斷個性化的連續時間軌跡在根本上是病態的。現有方法強迫做出嚴格的妥協：序列模型（例如潛在常微分方程）需要密集的縱向數據，而橫斷面方法（例如最佳運輸、基於流匹配的方法）則映射聚合人群，失去了個體動態。在本文中，我們展示了這種二分法可以被打破。我們介紹了CADENCE，一個原則性的概率框架，通過將潛在動態錨定到靜態的個體級上下文中，從孤立的快照中恢復連續的個體軌跡。我們提供了單時間點軌跡推斷的新識別性保證。通過結合基於分數的空間編碼器（雙射概率流常微分方程）以消除微分同構的歧義，並使用Soft Mixture-of-Experts（SMoE）路由器，我們顯示個體動態參數和路由函數是共同可識別的。在一系列基準測試中，涵蓋物理系統到現實世界生物數據，CADENCE在極度稀疏的快照上進行訓練，並具有上下文結構，其性能與在密集的完整軌跡數據上訓練的最先進序列模型相匹配或超過。
2605.23470.author: Christian Lagemann et.al.
2605.23470.authors: Christian Lagemann, Kai Lagemann, Steven L. Brunton, Sach Mukherjee
